verdicts.issue: Author field contains DBLP-style disambiguator '0001, Paul Barham' (literal string '0001' as 'surname'). Cosmetic noise, not a key/content issue.
Title is just 'LogGP' with subtitle field carrying the rest; renders fine in MIT Press style, no fix required.
Per bib-check.md §2 venue should read 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)' but currently lacks the '(NeurIPS)' suffix. Minor style nit, not broken.
Project-name key (concept name, not author surname). First author is Trinh, but key uses model name 'alphageometry' — intentional pattern per §5.
Already verified by claude-bib-audit-2026-05.
Title 'Understanding the mirai botnet' should protect 'Mirai' (proper noun) per §1: 'Understanding the {Mirai} botnet'. Minor.
Already verified by claude-bib-audit-2026-05.

Authors stored as initials only ('Asonov, D. and Agrawal, R.') but full names are publicly attested (Dmitri Asonov, Rakesh Agrawal). Per bib-check.md §4a, full first names should be used when canonically known. Also: key uses TitleCase with capital A and capital K (Asonov2004Keyboard) which deviates from project convention 'asonov2004keyboard'.

suggested_fix.author: Asonov, Dmitri and Agrawal, Rakesh
suggested_fix.note: Renaming key requires updating every [@Asonov2004Keyboard] reference in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd

verdicts.issue: Author field 'Services, Amazon Web' is reversed (treats 'Services' as surname, 'Amazon Web' as given). Should be a corporate author. Recommended: 'author = {{Amazon Web Services}}' (double-braced to prevent name parsing).
Per §2 NeurIPS venue should include '(NeurIPS)' suffix; entry uses bare 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems'. Minor.

Has TitleCase with underscore in key. Has @article type but the work is a UN-ITU report (not a journal article). Coexists with Balde2017 (book version of same content) — possible duplicate. Consider consolidating to one entry or differentiating clearly.

suggested_fix.note: Preferred: collapse with Balde2017 if both cite the same UN/ITU 2017 e-waste monitor; otherwise reclassify @article → @techreport. Renaming requires updating every [@Balde_2017] reference.

verdicts.issue: TitleCase key 'Balde2017' against project convention of lowercase keys; coexists with Balde_2017 (potential duplicate of same UN-ITU report). See Balde_2017 verdict for consolidation suggestion.
Single-letter author 'Baumann, R.' — published byline uses initial only, so acceptable per §4a, but full name 'Robert Baumann' is attested in IEEE author profiles.

Title field contains the encyclopedia name 'Wiley StatsRef: Statistics Reference Online' rather than the article topic (Kolmogorov-Smirnov). Cited as @misc — acceptable but a more specific title would be clearer.

Author list is incorrect/fabricated. Entry shows 'Zaharia, Matei and Chaudhury, Omar and McCann, Michael and others'. Actual blog post authors per BAIR are: Matei Zaharia, Omar Khattab, Lingjiao Chen, Jared Quincy Davis, Heather Miller, Chris Potts, James Zou, Michael Carbin, Jonathan Frankle, Naveen Rao, Ali Ghodsi. 'Chaudhury' and 'McCann' do not appear on the post.

suggested_fix.author: Zaharia, Matei and Khattab, Omar and Chen, Lingjiao and Davis, Jared Quincy and Miller, Heather and Potts, Chris and Zou, James and Carbin, Michael and Frankle, Jonathan and Rao, Naveen and Ghodsi, Ali

verdicts.issue: Entry classified as @article with journal='International Conference on Machine Learning' (a venue, not a journal). Per §3 ICML papers should be @inproceedings with publisher='PMLR' and full venue 'Proceedings of the 35th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML)'. Missing publisher field.

suggested_fix.booktitle: Proceedings of the 35th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML)

verdicts.issue: TitleCase key 'Biega2020Oper' against project lowercase convention; safe to leave but worth noting in any future style sweep.

Booktitle 'Microsoft Journal of Applied Research' does not exist (fabricated venue). Real publication is Microsoft Research Technical Report MSR-TR-2020-32 (May 2020 white paper). Entry should be @techreport with institution='Microsoft Research' and number='MSR-TR-2020-32'.
Key year 2022 vs entry date 2023-04-01 — DOI prefix is .2022. (arXiv submitted June 2022; IEEE TC issue published 2023). Within preprint-vs-publication 1-year tolerance per §5. No change needed.
Key year 2022 vs entry year 2021 — preprint Dec 2021, ICML 2022. Within §5 1-year tolerance.

Entry appears fabricated. Title 'Federated Learning and the Rise of Edge Intelligence: Challenges and Opportunities' by 'Brakerski, Zvika and others' in CACM vol 65 no 8 (Aug 2022) pp 54-63 does not match any real CACM publication. Zvika Brakerski is a cryptographer (BFV/BGV homomorphic encryption); no FL survey by him in CACM 2022 is locatable. CACM vol 65 no 8 actually contains different articles. Likely hallucinated citation.

suggested_fix.note: Recommend deleting this entry and replacing every [@brakerski2022federated] citation in book/quarto/contents/vol2/**/*.qmd with a verifiable FL survey (e.g., Kairouz et al. 2021 'Advances and Open Problems in Federated Learning'). Manual verification required before deletion.

verdicts.issue: Cited as @article with journal='ArXiv preprint' but also has publisher='ACM' and pages='619--633' which suggest a conference version. The arXiv 1806.05859 itself is a preprint; if a conference/journal version exists those fields should match it. Otherwise drop publisher and pages.
Single-initial author 'Brooks, R.' — published byline. Full name 'Rodney A. Brooks' is canonically known per §4a, consider expanding.
Duplicate of brown2020language (same paper 'Language Models are Few-Shot Learners', NeurIPS 2020). Both keys exist in the bib; one should be removed and citations consolidated.

suggested_fix.note: Drop brown2020gpt3 and update every [@brown2020gpt3] in book/quarto/contents/vol2/**/*.qmd to [@brown2020language] (or vice versa, choosing whichever key is more widely cited).

verdicts.issue: See brown2020gpt3 — these are the same paper; deduplicate.

Entry appears fabricated. Title 'Long-Term Software Support: A Key Factor in Sustainable AI Hardware' by 'Brown, Samantha' in journal 'Computer Ethics and Sustainability' vol 14 no 2 pp 112-130 (2021) cannot be located in any database (Google Scholar, Crossref, Semantic Scholar). 'Computer Ethics and Sustainability' is not a registered journal. Likely hallucinated.

suggested_fix.note: Recommend deleting and replacing citations with a verifiable source on long-term software support / sustainable AI hardware (e.g., Wu et al. 2022 'Sustainable AI: Environmental Implications, Challenges and Opportunities', arXiv 2111.00364). Manual verification required.

verdicts.issue: TitleCase key against project convention; cosmetic only.
Key year 2023 vs entry year 2024 — within §5 1-year tolerance (arXiv 2023, journal 2024).

Entry type @article but content is a chapter/section of the textbook 'Essentials of Electronic Testing for Digital, Memory, and Mixed-Signal VLSI Circuits' (Bushnell & Agrawal, 2000/2002). Should probably be @incollection or @inbook. Minor.
TitleCase key against project convention; cosmetic.
Key year 2009 vs entry year 2010 — within §5 1-year tolerance (preprint 2009, journal 2010).
Author field has DBLP-style '0001, Pratyush Mishra' / '0001, Yuankai Zhang' / '0001, David A. Wagner' disambiguators. Cosmetic noise.
